steps.title: Count the number of doublings
steps.content: From 1660 to 1760 is $1760-1660=100$ years. With doubling every 25 years, the count is $\tfrac{100}{25}=4$ doublings.

steps.title: Solve for the initial population
steps.content: Let $P_0$ be the 1660 population. Then $P_0\cdot 2^4=208{,}000$. $$P_0=\frac{208{,}000}{16}=13{,}000.$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
